texto: We need to make fun of and ridicule the media images that seek to keep us down, divide us against each other by age, class, and race, and insist that we spend so much psychic energy on our faces, clothes and bodies that nothing is left for ideas, social change, or politics.
explicacao: Citação de Susan Douglas sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “We need to make fun of and ridicule the media images that seek to keep us down, divide us against…” articula reflexao, cultural-politics, media em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como mudanca e termino.

frase_original: We need to make fun of and ridicule the media images that seek to keep us down, divide us against each other by age, class, and race, and insist that we spend so much psychic energy on our faces, clothes and bodies that nothing is left for ideas, social change, or politics.

texto: The recent spate of magazines for "parents" (i.e., mothers) bombard the anxiety-induced mothers of America with reassurances that they can (after a $100,000 raise and a personality transplant) produce bright, motivated, focused, fun-loving, sensitive, cooperative, confident, contented kids just like the clean, obedient ones on the cover.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Susan Douglas: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a motivacao. O trecho “The recent spate of magazines for "parents" (i.e., mothers) bombard the anxiety-induced mothers o…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que motivacao, motherhood, parenting pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de parenting.
